surface_verification.justification: Compliance grading-logic change; the affected surface is the Control-Grade markdown producer, not a runnable web/cli/api app. End-to-end verification = real adapter+scorer probe (baseline A; a 25-event honest maintenance sprint dropping recent FR-tag to 3% vs 19% keeps grade A) plus a 4964-test regression sweep.

behaviors.behavior: Workload composition (FR-tag decline) no longer penalises the requirement-traceability dimension
behaviors.evidence: end-to-end producer probe (maintenance sprint -> grade A); test_grade_gate.py::TestPerfectRepoIsA + test_low_requirement_traceability_is_a_gap_not_an_F PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Composition no longer caps the headline verdict below A
behaviors.evidence: probe: recent FR-tag 3% < all 19% -> still A (100/100), composition-neutral=True; test_grade_gate perfect repo has no 'Capped' in verdict

behaviors.behavior: Dashboard 'Recent changes traced' row is INFO, never WARN/frozen
behaviors.evidence: test_traceability.py::TestRenderTracedRow (lower-rate, 0%-recent, high-rate, all-no-FR) all assert INFO + no WARN

behaviors.behavior: No dead code / no stale reader of the removed functions/fields; docs + spec reflect the new behavior
behaviors.evidence: repo-wide ruff clean; F0 4964 passed; fresh-context adversarial opus review found zero remaining references + no blocking issues

degraded: F0 scope: ran every suite that consumes the compliance/grade machinery (compliance plugin 909 + 3 shared dirs 3881 + integration 174 = 4964). The other 12 plugin suites test untouched code. This is a plugin-SOURCE change (plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/lib/*), so `bash scripts/update-marketplace.sh` is required post-merge for the runtime plugin cache to pick it up; a WebUI re-grade (separate repo iterate) then lifts the WebUI B->A.
